Functions for scheduling, managing, and querying wakeup events in Pebble applications.

# Purpose
This C header file provides an interface for managing wakeup events in applications, allowing them to schedule tasks to be executed even when they are not actively running. It defines a function pointer type `WakeupHandler` for callback functions that handle wakeup events, and provides functions to subscribe to wakeup events, schedule new wakeup events, and cancel existing ones. The [`app_wakeup_schedule`](<#app_wakeup_schedule>) function allows applications to set up to eight wakeup events, with constraints on scheduling within a one-minute window of existing events. The file also includes functions to cancel individual or all wakeup events, retrieve information about wakeup events that triggered an application launch, and check the validity of scheduled wakeup events. The header relies on external dependencies such as `stdbool.h`, `stdint.h`, and other modules for time and wakeup services.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`
- `util/time/time.h`
- `services/normal/wakeup.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### app\_wakeup\_service\_subscribe<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_service_subscribe}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L40>)

Registers a callback for wakeup events.
- **Description**: Use this function to set a callback function that the system will call when a wakeup event occurs. This function must be called with a valid `WakeupHandler` to ensure the application can respond to wakeup events. If the handler is null, the function does nothing. Ensure that the application is prepared to handle the wakeup event when the callback is triggered.
- **Inputs**:
    - `handler`: A function pointer of type `WakeupHandler` that the system calls when a wakeup event occurs. Must not be null. If null, the function does not register a handler.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_service_subscribe`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_service_subscribe>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_wakeup\_schedule<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_schedule}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L55>)

Registers a wakeup event to trigger a callback at a specified time.
- **Description**: Use this function to schedule a wakeup event that will trigger a callback at a specified UTC time. This function is useful for applications that need to perform actions at specific times, even if they are not currently running. Ensure that the requested time is not in the past and that no other wakeup event is scheduled within one minute of the requested time. Applications can schedule up to 8 wakeup events. If the device is powered on after the scheduled time, and `notify_if_missed` is true, the user will be alerted about missed notifications. Handle potential errors such as scheduling conflicts, invalid arguments, resource limits, and internal system errors.
- **Inputs**:
    - `timestamp`: The requested time (UTC) for the wakeup event to occur. Must not be in the past.
    - `cookie`: An application-specific integer that represents the reason for the wakeup event.
    - `notify_if_missed`: A boolean indicating whether to notify the user if the wakeup event was missed due to the device being off.
- **Output**: Returns a `WakeupId` which is a non-negative identifier for the scheduled event. Negative values indicate errors: `E_RANGE` for scheduling conflicts, `E_INVALID_ARGUMENT` for past times, `E_OUT_OF_RESOURCES` for exceeding the event limit, and `E_INTERNAL` for system errors.
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_schedule`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_schedule>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_wakeup\_cancel<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_cancel}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L59>)

Cancels a scheduled wakeup event.
- **Description**: Use this function to cancel a previously scheduled wakeup event identified by its `wakeup_id`. This is useful when you no longer need the event to trigger or if you want to free up resources for other wakeup events. Ensure that the `wakeup_id` corresponds to a valid, scheduled event to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `wakeup_id`: Identifier of the wakeup event to cancel. Must correspond to a valid, scheduled wakeup event. Invalid or non-existent identifiers may lead to undefined behavior.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_cancel`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_cancel>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_wakeup\_cancel\_all<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_cancel_all}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L62>)

Cancels all scheduled wakeup events for the application.
- **Description**: Use this function to cancel all wakeup events that have been scheduled by the application. This is useful when the application needs to reset its wakeup schedule or when it is shutting down and no longer requires any scheduled wakeup events. Ensure that the application does not need any of the scheduled wakeup events before calling this function, as it will remove all of them without exception.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_cancel_all`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_cancel_all>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_wakeup\_get\_launch\_event<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_get_launch_event}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L70>)

Retrieves information about the wakeup event that launched the app.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain details about the wakeup event that caused the application to launch. This is useful for displaying information related to the wakeup event. The function must be called only if the application was launched due to a wakeup event. It returns true if the app was launched by a wakeup event, and false if not.
- **Inputs**:
    - `wakeup_id`: A pointer to a `WakeupId` variable where the function will store the ID of the wakeup event. Must not be null.
    - `cookie`: A pointer to an `int32_t` variable where the function will store the application-specific reason for the wakeup event. Must not be null.
- **Output**: Returns true if the app was launched due to a wakeup event, false otherwise.
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_get_launch_event`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_get_launch_event>)  (Implementation)


---
### app\_wakeup\_query<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_wakeup_query}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_wakeup.h#L81>)

Checks if a wakeup event is still scheduled.
- **Description**: Use this function to verify if a specific wakeup event, identified by `wakeup_id`, is still scheduled and valid. This function can also provide the scheduled time of the event if you supply a non-null `timestamp` pointer. It is useful for applications that need to manage or display information about scheduled wakeup events. Ensure that the `wakeup_id` is valid and corresponds to an event that was previously scheduled.
- **Inputs**:
    - `wakeup_id`: The identifier for the wakeup event to query. It must be a valid `WakeupId` that was previously scheduled.
    - `timestamp`: A pointer to a `time_t` variable where the scheduled time of the wakeup event will be stored. The time is in UTC. If you do not need this information, you can pass `NULL`. The function will not modify the value if `NULL` is passed.
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the wakeup event is still scheduled and valid, otherwise returns `false`. If `timestamp` is not `NULL`, it will be updated with the scheduled time of the event.
- **See Also**: [`app_wakeup_query`](<app_wakeup.c.md#app_wakeup_query>)  (Implementation)
